steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage has two parts joined by “However.” The first part states that the 'akeke'e has adapted to a well-defined habitat and has highly specialized traits. The second part uses “However” to introduce the downside: because it is highly specialized, it is especially vulnerable to environmental changes. The main idea must capture both the specialization and the resulting vulnerability.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Hawaiian birds display a unique range of physical and behavioral characteristics and as a result can only live in habitats unique to the Hawaiian archipelago.: This generalizes to all Hawaiian birds, ignoring the 'akeke'e's specific vulnerability described in the second half. ✗
  • Choice B — The 'akeke'e is an example of a species unique to the Hawaiian archipelago that is highly specialized and therefore particularly susceptible to habitat disturbances.: This covers both parts of the passage — the specialization and the resulting susceptibility. ✓
  • Choice C — The 'akeke'e is a species of bird that is related to many other Hawaiian birds but does not share a habitat with any of them.: The passage says nothing about the 'akeke'e not sharing a habitat with other birds, nor about its relatedness. ✗
  • Choice D — The 'akeke'e is an example of a highly specialized bird species found only on the Hawaiian archipelago and is related to several other highly specialized bird species found there.: The passage does not mention the 'akeke'e's relations to other species, and omits the vulnerability entirely. ✗
That matches Choice B — The 'akeke'e is an example of a species unique to the Hawaiian archipelago that is highly specialized and therefore particularly susceptible to habitat disturbances..
